## 4. Convergence Across Substrates {#sec-convergence}

<!-- STATUS: Stub — to be written.

Purpose: The biological parallel, tightened. Argue for convergence precisely,
concede limitations honestly, move on. Supporting evidence, not a core pillar.

Structure:

### The projection bottleneck as substrate-independent constraint {#sec-projection-bottleneck}
- Channel capacity (from Ch 3's sec-channel-capacity) makes the argument formal.
- Any system with internal representations exceeding output channel capacity
  must solve the same sequential coding problem.
- Evidence woven in, not separate subsections:
  - Vygotsky's inner/external speech: best-documented biological case. Brief.
    Inner speech compressed, predicative, semantically dense. External speech
    expanded for intersubjective recovery. @vygotsky_1934, @alderson-day_fernyhough_2015
  - Fodor's structural claim: output underdetermines internal representation.
    Widely shared. Revival via @quilty-dunn_etal_2023.
  - Speech bandwidth: ~39 bits/second, cross-linguistically invariant
    @reed_durlach_1998, @coupe_etal_2019. Internal bandwidth orders of magnitude
    higher. The bottleneck is quantifiable.
  - RSA convergence: @yamins_etal_2014, @kriegeskorte_2015, @schrimpf_etal_2021,
    @goldstein_etal_2022. Task-optimised DNNs predict biological neural responses.
    30-60% variance explained — real convergence with real limits.

### Convergence and its limits {#sec-convergence-limits}
- Convergence is structural, not mechanistic. Birds and aircraft.
- Co-evolution disanalogy (Deacon @deacon_1997): language and brain co-evolved;
  transformers adapt to language unidirectionally. RLHF partially but weakly
  closes the gap. The disanalogy is real and consequential.
- Levinson HEDGED: @levinson_2003, @levinson_etal_2002 — but challenged by
  @li_gleitman_2002. Use moderate claim: language biases default processing
  strategies, not "reshapes internal representation."
- What convergence explains (effectiveness less surprising than it appears)
  and what it doesn't (depth/robustness of text-only compression unresolved).

### The grounding problem as rate-distortion question {#sec-grounding-rate-distortion}
- Novel framing, no precedent. Flag as contribution.
- Does solving hierarchical rate-distortion problems require sensorimotor
  grounding? Cite @zaslavsky_etal_2018 as precedent for rate-distortion
  in linguistic categorisation.
- Present as open question, not established theory.

### Predictions {#sec-predictions-ch4}
- Prediction 8 only. RSA convergence (@pred-rsa-convergence): if convergence
  is deep, transformer representations should exhibit geometric properties
  similar to neural population codes. Systematic divergence would constrain
  the claim.

Target length: ~60% of v1 Section 4. Once channel capacity formalises the
constraint, the case is made quickly.

Key citations: @coupe_etal_2019, @goldstein_etal_2022, @quilty-dunn_etal_2023,
@li_gleitman_2002, @zaslavsky_etal_2018, @deacon_1997, @vygotsky_1934
-->
